title: Fix sqrt to preserve and halve Scalar dimensions
analysis: The sqrt function in reify-stdlib (line 18) uses `unary_f64(args, |x| Value::Real(x.sqrt()))` which extracts the f64 via `as_f64()` and wraps the result as `Value::Real`, discarding any Scalar dimension information. The fix requires two changes: (1) Add a `root(n: i8)` method to `DimensionVector` in reify-types that divides each rational exponent by n (e.g., root(2) halves exponents: AREA [2,0,...] → LENGTH [1,0,...]). (2) Replace the sqrt implementation in reify-stdlib to match on Scalar separately, computing `si_value.sqrt()` and `dimension.root(2)`, falling through to the existing Real behavior for non-Scalar numeric types. The `sanitize_value` function already handles NaN/inf for both Real and Scalar, so negative inputs will naturally produce Undef. DimensionVector already uses Rational exponents, so fractional exponents (e.g., LENGTH^(1/2)) are representable without any type changes. Existing tests for sqrt(Real) and sqrt(negative Real) already pass and should continue to pass unchanged.

steps.description: Write failing tests for DimensionVector::root() in crates/reify-types/src/dimension.rs: (a) root(2) of AREA → LENGTH (halve [2,0,...] to [1,0,...]), (b) root(2) of LENGTH^4 → LENGTH^2 (arbitrary even exponents), (c) root(2) of LENGTH → fractional exponent LENGTH^(1/2) with Rational(1,2), (d) root(2) of DIMENSIONLESS → DIMENSIONLESS

Implement DimensionVector::root(n: i8) method in crates/reify-types/src/dimension.rs. For each exponent Rational{num, den}, compute Rational::new(num, den * n). This divides each dimension exponent by n, producing fractional exponents when needed.

Write failing tests for dimension-aware sqrt in crates/reify-stdlib/src/lib.rs: (a) sqrt(Scalar{4.0, AREA}) must return Scalar{2.0, LENGTH}, (b) sqrt(Scalar{9.0, LENGTH^4}) must return Scalar{3.0, LENGTH^2}, (c) sqrt(Scalar{4.0, LENGTH}) must return Scalar{2.0, LENGTH^(1/2)} with fractional Rational exponent, (d) sqrt(Real(4.0)) must still return Real(2.0) — backward compat (existing test confirms), (e) sqrt(negative Scalar) must return Undef

Replace sqrt implementation in crates/reify-stdlib/src/lib.rs line 18. Change from `unary_f64(args, |x| Value::Real(x.sqrt()))` to use `unary(args, |v| ...)` which matches on Value::Scalar to preserve/halve dimensions via dimension.root(2), and falls through to as_f64() → Value::Real for Int/Real inputs. Apply sanitize_value to catch NaN from negative inputs.

design_decisions.decision: Add DimensionVector::root(n) rather than Mul trait on Rational
design_decisions.rationale: root(n) is the natural API for nth-root dimension operations (sqrt=root(2), cbrt=root(3)). It's more semantically clear than exposing general Rational multiplication. The implementation is trivial: divide each exponent by n via Rational::new(num, den*n). No need to add Mul for Rational since pow() already inlines the multiplication.

design_decisions.decision: Use unary() instead of unary_f64() for sqrt
design_decisions.rationale: unary_f64 extracts as_f64() too early, losing the Value variant information. Using unary() allows pattern matching on Value::Scalar to preserve dimension info, matching the existing pattern used by abs(). The non-Scalar fallback still uses as_f64() for backward compatibility.

design_decisions.decision: Rely on sanitize_value for negative input handling
design_decisions.rationale: (-x).sqrt() produces NaN in Rust, and sanitize_value already converts NaN/inf Scalars to Undef (line 103-105). No special-case needed for negative Scalar inputs — the existing defense-in-depth mechanism handles it uniformly for both Real and Scalar.

design_decisions.decision: Allow fractional dimension exponents from sqrt of odd-dimension values
design_decisions.rationale: sqrt(LENGTH) produces LENGTH^(1/2) which is physically valid (used in e.g., fracture toughness MPa·√m). Rational already supports this representation. Rather than erroring, we let the type system propagate the fractional exponent and let downstream dimension-checking catch any mismatches.

reuse.how: Use unary() instead of unary_f64() for sqrt, same pattern as abs() which also matches on Value variants to preserve Scalar dimensions
Already handles Scalar{NaN/inf} → Undef conversion, so negative Scalar inputs to sqrt are handled automatically without new code
root(2) of AREA uses Rational::new(2, 2) which auto-normalizes to Rational{1,1} = ONE, correctly producing LENGTH
Use in tests to construct Scalar inputs and verify expected output dimensions
Follow same match-on-Scalar pattern for sqrt: match Value::Scalar to transform si_value while preserving/transforming dimension
